title: EDL disabled vs enabled — side-by-side depth perception comparison
capture_instructions: Composite image. Left half: EDL disabled, RGB mode, Queens Campus at a 45-degree oblique angle showing buildings and roads. Right half: identical camera position, EDL enabled at Strength 0.5 Radius 2, same RGB mode. The difference in perceived depth and edge definition should be clearly visible — building edges, rooftop lips, and tree silhouettes should gain visible dark halos in the right panel.
ui_state: Left: EDL off. Right: EDL on (Strength 0.5, Radius 2). RGB color mode, oblique camera angle.

annotations.label: EDL disabled — flat appearance, depth ambiguous
EDL enabled — edges darkened, 3D structure immediately legible
Building edge — no depth halo without EDL
Same building edge — clear dark halo with EDL enabled
